items.description: Motorola is suing dozens of content creators and social media platforms including Instagram, X, and YouTube over posts it alleges are defamatory.

items.title: Airwallex is about to take on Stripe and the rest of the payments industry — in the physical world
items.description: Airwallex, valued at $8 billion, is launching a point-of-sale product that lets businesses accept in-person payments in multiple countries via a single platform.

items.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
items.description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

items.title: Sweden blames Russian hackers for attempting ‘destructive’ cyberattack on thermal plant
items.description: Sweden's minister for civil defense said Russian hackers are "now attempting destructive cyber attacks against organizations in Europe."

items.title: Snap is cutting 1,000 jobs, 16% of its workforce
items.description: The company cites advancements in AI for the cuts.

items.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
items.description: Reid Hoffman says tracking AI token use can gauge adoption, but cautions it should be paired with context and not treated as a direct productivity metric.

items.description: Adobe says the assistant can work across apps like Firefly, Photoshop, Premiere, Lightroom, Express, Illustrator and its other apps to do tasks for you.

items.title: This energy startup’s bet on 100-year-old grid tech is paying off
items.description: Ayr Energy is using a shortage of power conversion equipment to break open a decades-old market.

items.title: Gitar, a startup that uses agents to secure code, emerges from stealth with $9 million
items.description: The company uses AI to review code that, more often than not these days, has also been generated by AI.

items.title: This startup is betting tokenmaxxing will create the next compute giant
items.description: Parasail raised $32 million in a Series A, signaling a fractured future of models and compute.

items.title: HBO Max comes to India via exclusive JioHotstar deal
items.description: HBO Max will be available to JioHotstar subscribers as an add-on starting at ₹49 (about $0.50) per month, and would feature content from HBO, Max Originals, Warner Bros. Pictures, Warner Bros. Television, and DC Studios.

items.title: Fathom adds a bot-less meeting mode in a bid to take on Granola
items.description: Fathom will allow you to choose between video, audio-only, and transcription modes.

items.title: Spotify launches the ability to purchase physical books in the US and UK
items.description: Spotify users in the US and UK can officially purchase physical books through the Android app. iOS users will get the feature next week.

items.title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
items.description: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

items.title: AI data center startup Fluidstack in talks for $1B round at $18B valuation months after hitting $7.5B, says report
items.description: Fundraising is reportedly flowing for Fluidstack after it secured a $50 billion deal to build data centers for Anthropic.

items.title: Someone planted backdoors in dozens of WordPress plug-ins used in thousands of websites
items.description: Dozens of WordPress plug-ins were allegedly hijacked to push malware after they were sold to a new corporate owner.

items.title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
items.description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

items.title: London gets closer to its first robotaxi service as Waymo begins testing
items.description: Waymo's commercial service will eventually follow testing if the U.K. government approves.
